skill.name: markdown
skill.description: Use markdownlint-cli2 to check, fix, and verify Markdown formatting across documents with consistent rules and clear reporting.
skill.summary: Lint and fix Markdown documents with markdownlint-cli2 workflows.
skill.author: 46ki75

security_audit.summary: Static command findings are mostly false positives from Markdown code fences, not Ruby backtick execution. The skill still directs agents to run markdownlint-cli2 and shell file operations on documents, so publication is reasonable with a warning. No prompt injection, malicious network behavior, or cryptographic code was found.

medium_findings.title: External Command and File Modification Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run markdownlint-cli2, copy files, list files, and write a markdownlint configuration. This is legitimate for Markdown linting, but it can modify user documents or project configuration if paths are not scoped carefully.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly documented and intended for linting workflows. Risk is moderate because they can alter files, but no malicious command or exfiltration behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Ruby Backticks
low_findings.description: The static external command detections are triggered by Markdown fenced examples. The file contains documentation and command examples, not Ruby backtick execution code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown fences and examples in SKILL.md. No Ruby source file or executable backtick expression is present.

low_findings.title: Documentation Link Misclassified as Network Access
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to markdownlint rule documentation. The skill does not instruct agents to fetch, upload, or transmit data to that URL.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only URL appears in a documentation reference line. There is no network command, request API, or data transfer instruction nearby.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is Not Supported
low_findings.description: The static high-risk cryptography finding points to the YAML description area. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing operation, or security-sensitive primitive appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines only describe Markdown linting. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, encryption, signing, or password handling in the skill file.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command and File Modification Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs agents to run markdownlint-cli2, copy files, list files, and write a markdownlint configuration. This is legitimate for Markdown linting, but it can modify user documents or project configuration if paths are not scoped carefully.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly documented and intended for linting workflows. Risk is moderate because they can alter files, but no malicious command or exfiltration behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Markdown files often drift from style rules and become harder to review. This skill uses markdownlint-cli2 to fix common issues and report remaining structural problems.
actual_capabilities: Runs markdownlint-cli2 auto-fix commands for one or more Markdown files.
Checks remaining markdownlint errors after automated fixes complete.
Guides manual repairs for headings, emphasis headers, and long lines.
Creates or applies markdownlint configuration files for rule adjustments.
Reports initial errors, automatic fixes, manual fixes, and final status.
limitations: Requires markdownlint-cli2 to be available in the execution environment.
Cannot decide document hierarchy without user or project context.
May change formatting in files unless paths and scope are reviewed first.
Does not validate links, grammar, facts, or generated documentation accuracy.

use_cases.title: Prepare Pull Request Documentation
use_cases.description: Clean Markdown files before review so style problems do not distract from technical changes.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Docs
use_cases.description: Apply consistent Markdown rules across guides, tutorials, and reference documents.

use_cases.title: Validate Generated Notes
use_cases.description: Check release notes or planning documents for common Markdown formatting issues.

prompt_templates.title: Check One File
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner validation of a single document
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to check README.md with markdownlint-cli2. Summarize the errors and do not modify the file yet.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Markdown Formatting
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to auto-fix docs/guide.md, then run markdownlint-cli2 again and summarize any remaining issues.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Project Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill with the project markdownlint config to fix all Markdown files under docs. Report every file changed.

prompt_templates.title: Review Complex Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to lint the documentation set, manually resolve heading hierarchy issues, and explain each structural decision.

output_examples.input: A README has inconsistent heading levels, long lines, and trailing spaces.
output_examples.output: The report states that automatic fixes removed trailing spaces, then lists remaining heading and line length decisions.

output_examples.input: A docs folder needs formatting before a release branch is merged.
output_examples.output: The report lists changed files, final lint status, and any rules that needed project-specific configuration.

output_examples.input: A generated guide contains several bold lines that should be section titles.
output_examples.output: The response identifies each emphasis header and explains the chosen Markdown heading level.

best_practices: Run a check before auto-fix when users need to review planned changes.
Use project markdownlint configuration before creating new rule settings.
Verify the final file state with markdownlint-cli2 after manual edits.
anti_patterns: Do not run broad recursive fixes without confirming the target directory.
Do not silence rules just to hide problems that need structural review.
Do not rewrite document hierarchy without understanding the intended outline.

faq.question: Does this skill require markdownlint-cli2?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow depends on markdownlint-cli2 commands being available in the environment.

faq.question: Can it fix every markdownlint issue automatically?
faq.answer: No. Some issues require judgment, especially heading hierarchy and emphasis used as headings.

faq.question: Will it modify my files?
faq.answer: It can modify files when auto-fix is requested. Ask for a check-only pass when you need review first.

faq.question: Can it use a custom markdownlint configuration?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports existing config files and explains common rule adjustments.

faq.question: Does it check spelling or factual accuracy?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on Markdown style, formatting, and markdownlint rule compliance.

faq.question: Is it suitable for generated documentation?
faq.answer: Yes, when the generated files should follow markdownlint rules and project formatting standards.
